name: azure-resource-manager-sql-dotnet
description: Management plane SDK for provisioning and managing Azure SQL resources via Azure Resource Manager in .NET applications.
capabilities: Create and configure Azure SQL servers with authentication settings
Provision SQL databases with SKU selection and backup configuration
Manage elastic pools for cost-effective multi-database scenarios
Configure firewall rules and network access settings
Set up failover groups for high availability
List and manage SQL resources across subscriptions
author: sickn33
securitySummary: This is a prompt-only documentation skill with no executable code. It provides instructions for using Azure.ResourceManager.Sql SDK for management plane operations. The skill teaches proper security practices including DefaultAzureCredential for authentication and TLS 1.2 minimum version. Static analysis scanned 0 files with risk score 0/100. No security concerns identified.
valueStatement: Managing Azure SQL infrastructure manually through the portal is time-consuming and error-prone. This skill enables automated provisioning and management of SQL servers, databases, and elastic pools using the Azure.ResourceManager.Sql SDK.
userTitle: Provision Azure SQL Resources with .NET SDK
limitations: Management plane only - cannot execute SQL queries or stored procedures
Requires Azure subscription and appropriate RBAC permissions
Long-running operations require WaitUntil.Completed or manual polling
Does not replace Microsoft.Data.SqlClient for data plane operations

useCases.title: DevOps Engineer Automating Infrastructure
useCases.description: Automate SQL server and database provisioning as part of CI/CD pipelines using .NET applications

useCases.title: Developer Building Management Tools
useCases.description: Create internal tools for self-service database provisioning and resource management

useCases.title: Cloud Architect Designing Solutions
useCases.description: Implement infrastructure-as-code patterns for Azure SQL resources with proper security configuration

promptTemplates.title: Create SQL Server
promptTemplates.prompt: Create an Azure SQL server named 'prod-sql-server' in East US with TLS 1.2 minimum version and administrator login configured.

promptTemplates.title: Create Database in Elastic Pool
promptTemplates.prompt: Create a SQL database named 'app-db' in the existing elastic pool 'shared-pool' with Standard S0 SKU and 2GB max size.

promptTemplates.title: Configure Firewall Rules
promptTemplates.prompt: Add firewall rules to allow Azure services and the corporate IP range 203.0.113.0/24 to access the SQL server.

promptTemplates.title: Complete Infrastructure Setup
promptTemplates.prompt: Provision a complete SQL infrastructure: server with AAD admin, elastic pool with 100 eDTUs, three databases in the pool, firewall rules for Azure services and specific IPs, and enable audit logging.

bestPractices: Use DefaultAzureCredential for authentication - supports managed identity, CLI, and environment credentials
Use WaitUntil.Completed for operations that must finish before dependent actions
Configure firewall rules and network settings before attempting database connections
antiPatterns: Do not hardcode administrator passwords - use Azure Key Vault or managed identity
Do not use this SDK for executing queries - use Microsoft.Data.SqlClient instead
Avoid creating databases without elastic pools when managing multiple databases - pools reduce costs

faq.question: What is the difference between management plane and data plane?
faq.answer: Management plane (this SDK) creates and manages SQL resources like servers and databases. Data plane (Microsoft.Data.SqlClient) executes queries and stored procedures against those databases.

faq.question: How do I authenticate to Azure?
faq.answer: Use DefaultAzureCredential which automatically tries managed identity, Azure CLI, Visual Studio credentials, and environment variables in order.

faq.question: How long do provisioning operations take?
faq.answer: Server creation typically takes 5-15 minutes. Database creation takes 1-5 minutes. Use WaitUntil.Completed to wait for operations to finish.

faq.question: Can I use this skill for Azure SQL Managed Instance?
faq.answer: No, this SDK is for Azure SQL Database single databases and elastic pools. Managed Instance uses a different resource type.

faq.question: What authentication methods are supported for SQL server admin?
faq.answer: SQL authentication (username/password) and Azure Active Directory authentication. AAD auth is recommended for production workloads.

faq.question: How do I handle errors from the Azure Resource Manager API?
faq.answer: Catch RequestFailedException and check the Status property for HTTP codes like 400 (bad request), 409 (conflict), or 403 (forbidden).
